caption: Step 1: Open Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace
description: Open Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace
narration: And... action. Let's start by opening Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace. Every good walkthrough needs an establishing shot, and this is ours.

caption: Step 2: Split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right
description: Split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right. A split view like this is basically free cinematography for a tutorial.

caption: Step 3: Write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box
description: Write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box. Making the query visible is what turns the demo into a repeatable system.

caption: Step 4: Run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers
description: Run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers
narration: Quick scene change. We run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers. Cue the detective soundtrack; now the data gets to make its entrance.

caption: Step 5: Use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results
description: Use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results
narration: Cue the montage. We use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results. This is where the workflow earns its applause.

caption: Step 6: Click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type
description: Click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type. A visible click here helps the viewer track the action instantly.

caption: Step 7: Open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes
description: Open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes. Every good walkthrough needs an establishing shot, and this is ours.

caption: Step 8: Switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods
description: Switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods
narration: Quick scene change. We switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods. Sometimes the smartest move is just a better camera angle, and this is one of those moments.

caption: Step 9: Replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness
description: Replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness
narration: Cue the montage. We replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness. A small swap here shows how reusable the flow really is.

caption: Step 10: EV charging points rendered; now compare spread versus healthcare locations
description: EV charging points rendered; now compare spread versus healthcare locations
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. EV charging points are now on the map, so we can compare spread versus healthcare locations. Seeing both patterns on screen is what makes the comparison click.

caption: Step 11: Run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary
description: Run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary. This is the button where all the setup finally pays off.

caption: Step 12: Add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison
description: Add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison
narration: Quick scene change. We add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison. It is a nice reminder that the workflow can grow without turning brittle.

caption: Step 13: Single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets
description: Single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets
narration: Cue the montage. We single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 14: Open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows
description: Open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows. Now the viewer knows exactly where the story starts.

caption: Step 15: Export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools
description: Export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools. This is the handoff point from browser workflow to reusable output.

caption: Step 16: Tutorial complete: from iterative query editing to exportable, reproducible geo-data
description: Tutorial complete: from iterative query editing to exportable, reproducible geo-data
narration: That is the hero shot. The flow is captured end to end and ready for a replay. The flow is now captured end to end and ready to replay or adapt.
